$SOFI is reportedly expanding into the U.K. by acquiring most of PrimaryBid which is a British fintech platform focused on retail investor access to IPOs.

PrimaryBid previously raised $250M from backers including SoftBank and LSEG.

Demand for AI compute is still eating every credible source of chip supply alive.

$NVTS is expanding into India through Cyient’s new GaN power IC launch built on Navitas 650V platform.

The partnership targets AI data centers, telecom, chargers and EV infrastructure while adding a second source for select Navitas GaN products.

$ASTS achieved a 98.9 Mbps peak download speed from space directly to an unmodified smartphone using previous-generation Block 1 satellites.

Next-gen Block 2 satellites are expected to double those speeds bringing space-based cellular broadband closer to real commercial scale.

• Revenue $351M vs Est. $339M
• EPS $1.15 vs Est. $0.88

FY26 Guidance

Piper Sandler says $TSLA investors are effectively getting Optimus “for free” at $400/share.

Their updated model says Tesla’s 17 existing product lines already justify roughly today’s stock price which excludes the humanoid robot business entirely.

The capital will help fund AI infrastructure expansion.

By the way, the opposite was true from 2009 to 2020, paychecks (orange) outpaced CPI/prices (blue). So those without assets could at least "hold serve."

8/12

53% likely have no assets (no stocks and rent), so they are not benefiting from the rise in asset prices (booming stocks and soaring home prices). 

They have to make ends meet with their paycheck alone, and they cannot.

nitter.net/drboycewatkins1/status…

7/12

Prices have the public so upset that consumer sentiment, as measured by the University of Michigan, is approaching nearly a one-century low.

Why now?  Since the end of COVID, the cumulative increase in prices (blue) is outpacing their paycheck (orange).

4/12
What does negative real wages mean?  

The top panel is the University of Michigan Consumer Sentiment Index. The red part of the line shows when real wages are negative (bottom panel, same as the chart above).

When real wages go negative, consumer sentiment gets crushed.

Amazon on capex: “The faster AWS grows, the more short-term CapEx we will spend. AWS has to lay out cash for land, power, buildings, chips, servers and networking gear in advance of when we can monetize it, typically 6 to 24 months before we start billing customers depending on the component.”
